```{python}
import geopandas as gpd
from joblib import Parallel, delayed
import xarray as xr
from xarray.core.dataarray import DataArray
import networkx as nx
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import rioxarray
from shapely import remove_repeated_points
from shapely.errors import GEOSException
from shapely.geometry import shape, Point, LineString
from shapely.ops import linemerge
from skimage import morphology
from sklearn import linear_model
from tqdm import tqdm
#import ruptures as rpt
from pathlib import Path
import argparse
import json
import math
import rasterio as rasterio
from rasterio import features
from rasterio.mask import mask
from rasterio.plot import show
from rasterio.plot import show_hist
#import odc.geo.xr
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pysheds
#from osgeo import gdal
from pysheds.grid import Grid 
import mplleaflet
%matplotlib inline
import pyproj
from pysheds.view import Raster, ViewFinder
import matplotlib.colors as colors
pd.options.mode.chained_assignment
print('successfully imported all packages')
```

# Reading in DEM
```{python}
grid = Grid.from_raster("/Users/dillencorlett/pbr/data/watershedDEM.tif")
dem_sheds = grid.read_raster("/Users/dillencorlett/pbr/data/watershedDEM.tif")
```

Optional code to inspect DEM:
```{python}
dem_sheds.viewfinder
```

```{python}
dem_sheds[dem_sheds == -999999.0] = 0
```

```{python}
dem_view = grid.view(dem_sheds)

#dem_view.viewfinder

plt.imshow(grid.view(dem_view), cmap='terrain', zorder=1)
```

# Hydroconditioning
Filling pits in DEM:
```{python}
pit_filled_dem = grid.fill_pits(dem_sheds)
pits = grid.detect_pits(pit_filled_dem)
assert not pits.any()
```

Filling depressions in DEM:
```{python}
flooded_dem = grid.fill_depressions(pit_filled_dem)
depressions = grid.detect_depressions(flooded_dem)
assert not depressions.any()
```

Resolving flats in DEM:
```{python}
inflated_dem = grid.resolve_flats(flooded_dem)
flats = grid.detect_flats(inflated_dem)
#assert not flats.any()
```

For some reason the code above returns an AssertionError, but plotting flats (as seen below) verifies that there are no (easily visible) flats remaining, so I think we can move on to flow direction.

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,6))
fig.patch.set_alpha(0)

plt.imshow(flats, cmap='Greys_r', zorder=1)
plt.title('Flats', size=14)
plt.tight_layout()
```

Generating flow direction:
```{python}
fdir = grid.flowdir(inflated_dem)
```

Generating flow accumulation:
```{python}
acc = grid.accumulation(fdir)
```

Visual check of flow accumulation:
```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,6))
fig.patch.set_alpha(0)
im = ax.imshow(acc, zorder=2,
               cmap='cubehelix',
               norm=colors.LogNorm(1, acc.max()),
               interpolation='bilinear')
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label='Upstream Cells')
plt.title('Flow Accumulation', size=14)
plt.tight_layout()
```

Importing the watershed boundary as a vector to clip the DEM to:

```{python}
watershed = gpd.read_file("/Users/dillencorlett/pbr/data/bartleson_watershed.geojson")
```

```{python}
from scipy.ndimage import binary_erosion

# Reproject watershed to match DEM CRS if needed
watershed = watershed.to_crs(grid.crs.srs)
```

```{python}
# Rasterize watershed polygon to match DEM grid (1 = inside, 0 = outside)
watershed_mask = features.rasterize(
    [geom for geom in watershed.geometry],
    out_shape=grid.shape,
    fill=0,
    transform=grid.affine,
    all_touched=False,
    default_value=1,
    dtype=np.uint8
).astype(bool)
```

```{python}
# Mask flow accumulation to watershed
acc_masked = np.where(watershed_mask, acc, np.nan)

# Visual check
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
im = ax.imshow(acc_masked, cmap='cubehelix',
               norm=colors.LogNorm(1, np.nanmax(acc_masked)),
               interpolation='bilinear')
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label='Upstream Cells')
plt.title('Flow Accumulation (Clipped to Watershed)', size=14)
plt.tight_layout()
```

```{python}
# Get 1-cell-wide watershed boundary ring via erosion
inner = binary_erosion(watershed_mask, structure=np.ones((3, 3)), border_value=0)
boundary_ring = watershed_mask & ~inner
```

```{python}
# Restrict candidates to likely stream cells (top 5% accumulation)
valid_acc = acc_masked[np.isfinite(acc_masked)]
stream_thresh = np.percentile(valid_acc, 95)
candidates = boundary_ring & (acc_masked >= stream_thresh)

# Fallback if threshold is too strict
if not np.any(candidates):
    candidates = boundary_ring & np.isfinite(acc_masked)

# Pick boundary cell with max accumulation
best_idx = np.nanargmax(np.where(candidates, acc_masked, np.nan))
row, col = np.unravel_index(best_idx, acc_masked.shape)
pour_acc = float(acc_masked[row, col])

# Convert to geographic coordinates (cell center)
x, y = rasterio.transform.xy(grid.affine, row, col)
print(f"Estimated pour point at (x={x:.4f}, y={y:.4f}), accumulation={pour_acc:.0f} cells")
```

Save the outlet as a point GeoJSON:
```{python}
gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame({"acc":[float(pour_acc)]}, geometry=[Point(x, y)], crs=grid.crs.srs)
gdf.to_file("estimated_pour_point.geojson", driver="GeoJSON")
```
